# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy application files
COPY . /app

# Install dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gcc && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt && \
    apt-get remove -y gcc && \
    apt-get autoremove -y && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set environment variables
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# Run the application
CMD ["python", "app.py"]
